dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 8515 IH: Consumer Price Information Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Ms. Letlow introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on the Judiciary

form.official-title: To require the publication of a Consumer Price Information Statement for each major rule, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Consumer Price Information Act of 2026.

paragraph.text: Major rules may impact consumer prices for essential goods and services.
Clear communication regarding any such impact can improve public understanding and support informed policymaking.
Providing consistent consumer price information promotes transparency and trust in government decision-making.

subsection.text: For any major rule issued by the head of an agency, the issuing agency shall publish a Consumer Price Information Statement as described in subsection (c).
Each Consumer Price Information Statement required by subsection (a) shall include the following:

paragraph.text: A qualitative or quantitative description of any potential impact of the relevant major rule on consumer prices for the following:

subparagraph.text: Energy, including electricity, gasoline, and natural gas.

paragraph.text: An identification of populations that may be more sensitive to any such impact, including the following:
A description of any regional variation with respect to any such impact, where applicable.
A summary of key assumptions and methodologies used to prepare the Consumer Price Information Statement.

subsection.text: Each Consumer Price Information Statement required by subsection (a), at the time the relevant major rule is proposed, shall be—

paragraph.text: published in the Federal Register with such major rule; and
made publicly available on the website of the issuing agency.
The term agency has the meaning given that term in section 551 of title 5, United States Code.
The term major rule has the meaning given that term in section 804 of title 5, United States Code.

subsection.text: No additional amounts are authorized to be appropriated to carry out this Act, which shall be carried out solely using amounts otherwise authorized to be appropriated or otherwise made available to agencies.
This section shall apply beginning on the date that is 60 days after the date of the enactment of this Act.
